article_title: FDA Reverses Course on UniQure's Huntington's Gene Therapy, Demanding Sham Brain Surgery Trial That Could Delay Treatment for Years
summary: Well-reported article on an important regulatory development. The FierceBiotech 403 is a Cloudflare bot-block, not a dead link — the article exists and is corroborated across multiple live sources. Content changes required: address anatomical discrepancy in Makary's quote, moderate the title, and clarify the data source for Phase I/II statistics.

findings.message: HTTP 403 on FierceBiotech URL is a Cloudflare bot-challenge, not a dead link. Article confirmed to exist via web search and corroborating sources. URL is valid.

Anatomical discrepancy in Makary quote section. The article states AMT-130 is injected into the striatum (correct per trial design), but Makary's quote refers to injection 'intrathecally into the ventricle' — these are distinct anatomical locations. The article does not address this discrepancy. Either Makary misspoke/referenced a different therapy, or the article should note the mismatch between his anatomical description and AMT-130's actual delivery route.
findings.details: Body section 'The Makary Factor' quotes Makary describing injection 'intrathecally into the ventricle' while the 'What AMT-130 Is' section correctly states the therapy is injected into the striatum via stereotactic neurosurgery.

findings.message: The Phase I/II statistics (cUHDRS 75% slowing, p=0.003; TFC 60% slowing, p=0.033; CSF NfL -8.2%) are attributed to the March 2 GlobeNewswire press release, but that release does not contain those numerical details — they were from the September 2025 data disclosure. The attribution should reference the original data release or clearly distinguish between the two disclosures.
findings.details: The March 2 GlobeNewswire press release (the cited source) provides only the regulatory update narrative. Detailed efficacy statistics come from the September 2025 Phase I/II data announcement.

findings.message: Title contains editorial framing ('That Could Delay Treatment for Years') that goes beyond factual reporting. While the article body appropriately hedges ('likely add years'), the title presents the delay as near-certain rather than contingent on trial design and enrollment outcomes.
findings.details: Title: 'FDA Reverses Course on UniQure's Huntington's Gene Therapy, Demanding Sham Brain Surgery Trial That Could Delay Treatment for Years'

content_preview.title: FDA Reverses Course on UniQure's Huntington's Gene Therapy, Demanding Sham Brain Surgery Trial That Could Delay Treatment for Years
content_preview.summary: The FDA rejected uniQure's Phase I/II data for AMT-130 and now demands a sham surgery-controlled Phase 3 trial, reversing its own prior guidance and sending shares down 40 percent.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The U.S. Food and Drug Administration has reversed its prior regulatory guidance on uniQure's AMT-130, a one-time gene therapy for Huntington's disease, telling the company that its Phase I/II trial data are insufficient to support a marketing application and demanding instead a prospective, randomized, double-blind trial in which control patients would undergo sham brain surgery. The announcement, disclosed by [uniQure on March 2](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2026/03/...

recommendations: 1 source(s) could not be verified — check URLs before resubmitting

editor_notes.content_quality: The article is well-structured and covers the regulatory event comprehensively. It correctly identifies the key elements: the FDA's reversal, the ethical sham surgery dilemma, the Makary connection, market reaction, and open questions. The 'What We Don't Know' section is particularly strong — it frames unknowns transparently without speculating. Word count (1,122) is appropriate for the News category. The sourcing is solid with three major biotech trade publications plus the official press release.
editor_notes.source_verification: Three of four sources are confirmed live and authoritative (GlobeNewswire official press release, STAT News, BioPharma Dive). The FierceBiotech URL returned HTTP 403 due to Cloudflare bot-challenge protection — manual verification via web search confirms the article exists with a matching title and content corroborated by other sources. The 403 is a server-side bot-blocking issue, not evidence of a dead link. The FierceBiotech source may be replaced with the BioSpace article (https://www.biospace.com/fda/uniqure-eyes-phase-3-for-huntingtons-gene-therapy-despite-makarys-apparent-criticism) which covers the same Makary quote and analyst reaction.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Core facts are accurate: the FDA's demand for a sham surgery-controlled Phase 3 trial, the prior Breakthrough Therapy and RMAT designations, the ~40% stock decline, the Type A meeting on January 30 2026, and the planned Type B meeting in Q2 2026. One factual issue requires correction: the Makary quote section does not address the anatomical inconsistency between his description ('intrathecally into the ventricle') and AMT-130's actual delivery route (into the striatum via stereotactic neurosurgery). These are distinct brain regions. The article must either note that Makary's anatomical description does not match AMT-130's documented procedure — raising the question of whether he was referencing this therapy at all — or confirm this discrepancy based on available reporting. Additionally, the Phase I/II statistics are attributed to the March 2 press release, which contains only the regulatory narrative; the numerical data originated from the September 2025 trial results announcement. Attribution should be corrected or clarified.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: The article body maintains appropriate neutrality, presenting the FDA's position, uniQure's response, analyst commentary, and the ethical debate without editorializing. The Makary section appropriately notes the conflict between his description and the published data without rendering a verdict. However, the title — 'That Could Delay Treatment for Years' — introduces editorial framing not found in the body. The article itself uses 'likely add years' as a hedged characterization; the title should match this conditional language rather than implying near-certainty.
editor_notes.originality: The article synthesizes multiple primary and trade sources into a coherent narrative that goes beyond wire-service repetition. The ethical framing of the sham surgery dilemma and the Leerink analyst observation about the internal contradiction in the FDA's position add analytical value. The 'What We Don't Know' section demonstrates genuine journalistic judgment.
concerns: Anatomical discrepancy in Makary quote not addressed: he describes intrathecal/ventricular injection; AMT-130 is delivered to the striatum. This gap must be explicitly acknowledged or explained — it is material to evaluating whether Makary was even referring to AMT-130.

Phase I/II statistics attributed to the March 2 GlobeNewswire release, which does not contain those figures. The September 2025 data disclosure is the correct source and should be cited or the attribution clarified.
Title uses 'That Could Delay Treatment for Years' as though delay is near-certain, which overstates confidence and is inconsistent with the body's hedged language.
recommendations: Add a sentence in 'The Makary Factor' section noting that Makary's description of 'intrathecal/ventricular' injection does not match AMT-130's documented striatal delivery route, and that this raises questions about whether his comments were directed at this therapy specifically.

Correct or clarify the attribution for the Phase I/II efficacy statistics (cUHDRS, TFC, CSF NfL figures) to reference the September 2025 data announcement rather than the March 2 regulatory update press release.

Revise the title to use conditional language consistent with the body — for example: 'FDA Reverses Course on UniQure's Huntington's Gene Therapy, Demanding Sham Surgery Trial in Setback That Could Add Years to Approval Timeline'.
Replace or supplement the FierceBiotech source with a reliably accessible URL such as the BioSpace article covering the same Makary quote and analyst commentary.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Solid, substantive journalism on an important FDA regulatory reversal with genuine public health implications. The article demonstrates command of the clinical and regulatory complexity. Three targeted corrections are required — addressing the Makary anatomical discrepancy, fixing the statistics attribution, and moderating the title — before publication.
